ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn code exists specifically for a peppermint-spearmint-tarragon blend tea. FOODON_03301103 (herbal tea food product) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Herbal infusions) is the closest parent. No specific code exists for a mint blend tea product.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 229953004 (Herbal tea) is the closest parent concept. No specific code for peppermint-spearmint blend tea exists in SNOMED CT.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard tall cup (approximately 354 ml / 354 g) as served at Starbucks Singapore, with no added sugar.

nutrient_source.title: Peppermint tea, brewed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 173474); Spearmint tea, brewed — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 173476); values represent plain brewed herbal mint tea per 100 g
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA entry exists for a peppermint-spearmint-tarragon blend. Values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy brewed peppermint tea (FDC ID 173474) as the primary analogue, which is a plain water-based herbal infusion with negligible macronutrients. The HPB Singapore Foodo database does not carry this specific Starbucks product. The product is endorsed by the Health Promotion Board Singapore as 'no added sugar', confirming zero sugar content. Nutrient values reflect plain brewed herbal mint tea without milk or sweeteners.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of herbal teas and non-caloric beverages — University of Sydney GI Database; plain herbal infusions with negligible carbohydrate content
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Mint Blend Tea. Plain herbal teas and non-caloric infusions are universally classified as GI=1 (effectively zero) by convention in the University of Sydney GI Database framework, as they contain no glycemic carbohydrates. glycemic_index set to 1 (the lowest valid positive value, effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 because carbohydrate_g per serving is negligible (0.2 g per 100 g × 3.54 serving = 0.71 g total, GL rounds to 0.0).

hypertension.key_factor: Negligible sodium content (2 mg per 100 g); no added salt
hypertension.guidance: Mint Blend Tea is suitable for individuals with hypertension as it contains negligible sodium (approximately 7 mg per 354 ml serving) and no added sugar, making it a safe hydration choice that does not contribute to blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: No added sugar; negligible carbohydrate content; GI effectively zero
type2_diabetes.guidance: Mint Blend Tea is appropriate for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contains no added sugar (HPB-endorsed), has a negligible carbohydrate load (approximately 0.7 g per 354 ml serving), and an effectively zero glycemic index, posing no meaningful postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, no added sugar
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Mint Blend Tea presents no cardiovascular risk factors as it contains zero fat, zero saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and no added sugar, and may be freely consumed as part of a heart-healthy dietary pattern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Mint Blend Tea is a herbal infusion beverage served at Starbucks Singapore, composed of a blend of peppermint leaves, spearmint leaves, and a touch of tarragon. The drink is prepared by steeping these dried or fresh herbs in hot water, producing a bright, refreshing, and aromatic beverage. Peppermint contributes a cool, piercing menthol flavour; spearmint adds a sweeter, milder minty note; and tarragon introduces a subtle anise-like or licorice undertone. The resulting flavour profile is described as creamy, herbaceous, and multi-dimensional. The product is endorsed by the Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore under the 'no added sugar' designation, confirming it contains no added sweeteners. As a plain herbal infusion, it is essentially calorie-free (approximately 1 kcal per 100 g), contains no fat, no protein, and negligible carbohydrates, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. The beverage is sold at Starbucks Singapore outlets, including the One Fullerton location, at approximately SGD 6.20 per serving.
common_user_queries: What is Mint Blend Tea at Starbucks Singapore?
Is Mint Blend Tea suitable for diabetics?
How many calories in Starbucks Mint Blend Tea?
Is Mint Blend Tea HPB endorsed?
What herbs are in Starbucks Mint Blend Tea?
What does Mint Blend Tea taste like?
Is Mint Blend Tea good for hypertension?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central for this specific Starbucks Mint Blend Tea product. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy brewed peppermint tea (FDC ID 173474) as the closest approved analogue. The HPB 'no added sugar' endorsement confirms zero sugar. GI is set by convention for non-caloric herbal infusions. Confidence is medium due to the adapted (not exact) source match.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero as this is a plain herbal infusion containing no protein-bearing ingredients in meaningful quantities after brewing. 2. fat_g is naturally zero by the nature of a water-based herbal tea infusion. 3. sugar_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore 'no added sugar' endorsement. 4. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero as the plant material is not consumed, only the infused liquid. 5. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 since the carbohydrate content per serving is negligible. 6. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy brewed peppermint tea entry. 7. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 8. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 9. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 10. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 11. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 12. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 13. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 14. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 15. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 16. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 17. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 18. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source. 19. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (English, Chinese Simplified, Chinese Traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the product is served in Singapore. 20. The cuisine field reflects the café/Western context of Starbucks Singapore. 21. energy_kcal of 1.0 per 100 g reflects the trace carbohydrate content of brewed herbal tea per USDA SR Legacy reference.
